A-share market-making — Strategy3 (interactive depth grid)

Short at prevClose×(1+spread), cover at next open · FX fixed 7.2 · backtest 2024-07-01 → 2026-06-18. Set each asset's quoted USD notional at every spread level below; charts 3 & 5 recompute instantly. Each asset's total is the sum across its spread levels.

Depth grid — USD notional quoted at each spread level (0 = not traded). Edit any cell to update charts 3 & 5 live.

Fees (bps / leg):  · Window: cumulative PnL re-bases to 0 per window

3  Per-asset total — pick a name from the dropdown

5  Correlation-weighted FXI hedge (dynamic, FXI only)

Idea. Instead of a flat FXI buy every night, size the FXI buy by how each asset's overnight return co-moves with FXI's overnight return, using a rolling correlation that updates daily. Two windows are shown: 21 and 60 trading days.

Returns. Asset overnight return ri(T) = openi(T)/closei(T−1)−1. FXI overnight return rFXI(T) = FXI(sell ≈ Beijing 08:00, T)/FXI(buy ≈ Beijing 16:00, T−1)−1.

Sizing & no look-ahead. FXI bought at 16:00 on day T−1 uses the rolling correlation through day T−1. FXI notional that night = F(T) = Σi ρi × Ni (signed ρ; Ni = asset i's deployed notional = sum of its grid row). All 10 assets included. PnL = F(T) × rFXI(T) − hedge fee/leg. The flat (S4) line uses notional = Σi Ni (your total deployed notional), so it rescales as you edit the grid.

Warm-up. The hedge = 0 until each window has enough observations (21d from day 22, 60d from day 61). The grey band marks up to the 60-day warm-up in the All view; the 6-month view is already warmed up.
